problem_statement: AI-assisted 'vibe coding' lets non-experts build sophisticated applications while the AI makes thousands of legally consequential micro-decisions (licensing, privacy, security) without human legal oversight, creating a mismatch between technical capacity and legal/compliance infrastructure.
magnitude_evidence: Perry et al.: 'participants who had access to an AI assistant wrote significantly less secure code than those without access,' with only 3% of AI-assisted developers implementing secure encryption compared to 22% without AI (Perry et al. 2785)
insufficiency_evidence: Traditional legal service delivery models—manual document review, hourly consultation, post-hoc compliance audits—cannot match AI-assisted development's velocity or solo entrepreneurship's economics; hourly legal rates of $300-800 place review beyond solo entrepreneurs' reach
solution_mechanisms: real-time compliance checks in dev workflow
automated legal document generation (ToS, privacy policy, incident response)
key_constructs: compliance at the speed of code
governance_refs: NIST Framework for Responsible AI Development
notes: Legal-practitioner opinion/position piece; not empirical study by author (cites others' empirical work). Governance_refs 'EU AI Act' not explicitly named—remove if strict; document cites GDPR/NIST/OWASP directly.
